name: commercetools Headless Commerce Platform API
what_it_does: API-first headless commerce platform — enabling enterprise retailers and brands to build composable commerce architectures with product catalog management, cart and order APIs, pricing, promotions, customer accounts, and inventory management, following MACH (Microservices, API-first, Cloud-native, Headless) principles, with the commerce backend fully decoupled from frontend presentation.
use_cases: Building headless e-commerce storefront via commercetools API for custom React/Next.js frontend
Managing complex product catalog with variants via commercetools for enterprise retail
Implementing B2B commerce with company accounts and custom pricing via commercetools API
Orchestrating multi-store commerce across regions via commercetools for global retail brands
Building composable commerce stack via commercetools as commerce backend with custom checkout
not_for: Small businesses needing out-of-box storefront (use Shopify or BigCommerce)
Teams without frontend development resources for custom UI (commercetools has no storefront)
Simple catalog scenarios without complex B2B or multi-region requirements
best_when: An enterprise retailer or brand needs a fully composable, API-first commerce backend — where commercetools provides product, pricing, cart, and order APIs that power custom frontends, mobile apps, IoT, and physical retail simultaneously.
avoid_when: You need a turnkey storefront without custom frontend development, are a small/mid business, or have simple single-channel commerce requirements.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with client credentials and authorization code flows. Scoped access: manage_products, view_orders, manage_customers, etc. Client ID + Secret for client credentials grant. Token endpoint: https://auth.commercetools.com/oauth/token. Project key in all API URLs. Webhooks for order/product/customer change events. Message subscriptions (SQS, SNS, Google Pub/Sub, Azure Service Bus). Separate frontend (anonymous) and backend (client credentials) auth flows.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; free trial sandbox available
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise licensing: custom GMV-based pricing (typically $100K+/year for significant deployments)
pricing.notes: German company (Munich). Founded 2006 by Dietmar Rietsch and Dirk Hoerig. Raised $140M+ total funding. IPO preparation (valued ~$1.9B 2021). Series D $140M led by Accel 2021. MACH Alliance founding member. Customers: Audi, Volkswagen, Lego, Sunrise (Swiss telecom), Bosch, Danone, Sephora. Multi-tenant SaaS with Project concept. Region: US-East, EU-West, AU. GraphQL added 2020. Composable Commerce methodology publisher. Import API for bulk data. ML pricing engine (Dynamic Pricing add-on). B2B features: Company accounts, business unit hierarchy, custom approval workflows. Multi-currency, multi-language, multi-tenant.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits by project; not publicly documented; enterprise limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST + GraphQL API. OAuth2 with fine-grained scopes. Project key in all URLs. Webhooks with message subscriptions (SQS/SNS/Pub/Sub). JS/TS/Java/.NET/PHP/Python SDKs. PCI-DSS/GDPR/SOC2/ISO27001. Excellent developer documentation. Message-based event subscriptions for reliable webhook delivery.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Optimistic concurrency via version field on all resources. All mutations require current version number — prevents race conditions. Idempotency-Key header supported. Cart/order operations idempotent with version checking.
known_agent_gotchas: Project key required in every API URL path: /project-key/products — not just in auth
Optimistic concurrency: all write operations require current `version` field — must fetch current version before update
Scoped OAuth2: request only the scopes needed — manage_products, view_orders, etc. separately
Message subscriptions (not HTTP webhooks) preferred for reliable event delivery — use SQS/SNS/Pub/Sub
GraphQL and REST APIs are feature-equivalent — GraphQL recommended for complex queries to reduce round trips
Trial sandbox is free but production requires annual enterprise contract

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. PCI-DSS, GDPR, SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2 with fine-grained scopes. Optimistic concurrency prevents race conditions. German company under strict EU data protection. US/EU/AU data residency. Message subscriptions for reliable event delivery (not HTTP webhooks that can fail). Customer PII GDPR-protected. Enterprise security review process.
